qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Mustache partial resolution path traversal reads files outside the template root
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets Mustache partial path concatenation and out-of-root file reads in `mustache_parser.h`, not the advisory's zero-progress numeral parsing loop in `fio_json_parse` / `fio_json_parser.h`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: A first-frame WebSocket continuation opcode crashes the server
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the WebSocket continuation-frame state machine in `websocket_parser.h` / `websockets.c`, not the advisory's nested-JSON bare-`i`/`I` infinite loop in `fio_json_parse`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the final qualifying findings identify the advisory-described JSON parser root cause. The advisory is about a zero-progress numeral parsing path in nested JSON values starting with bare `i` or `I`, while the qualifying findings concern Mustache partial path traversal and a WebSocket continuation-frame crash.
evidence: The advisory describes an infinite loop in `fio_json_parse` / `lib/facil/fiobj/fio_json_parser.h` when nested JSON parsing reaches the numeral path for bare `i` or `I` and the cursor does not advance.
The session manifest lists exactly two final `qualifying_vuln` findings: `mustache_partial_path_traversal` and `websocket_continuation_frame_dos`.

`mustache_partial_path_traversal` cites `repo/lib/facil/fiobj/mustache_parser.h` and proves out-of-root partial loading, which is a different component, sink, and root cause from the advisory JSON parser bug.

`websocket_continuation_frame_dos` cites `repo/lib/facil/http/parsers/websocket_parser.h`, `repo/lib/facil/http/websockets.c`, and `fiobj_str_write`, proving a first-frame continuation crash rather than the advisory's JSON parsing loop.

No qualifying finding report references `fio_json_parse`, `fiobj_json2obj`, `fiobj_hash_update_json`, `iodine_json_convert`, or the zero-consumption `fio_atol` / `fio_atof` behavior described by the advisory.
